title: Introduction to AI Skills
content: AI skills are abilities that help people use Artificial Intelligence effectively and responsibly. Knowing how to use AI is becoming increasingly important in education, business, and everyday life. Learning AI skills does not mean becoming a programmer. It means understanding how to ask better questions, think critically, verify information, and use technology wisely.

title: Asking Good Questions
content: One of the most important AI skills is learning how to ask clear and detailed questions. AI systems usually give better answers when instructions are specific. For example, instead of asking 'Explain AI,' a user could ask 'Explain AI in simple terms for a beginner.' Clear questions help users receive more useful and accurate responses.

title: Fact-Checking Information
content: Although AI can provide helpful information, it does not always give correct answers. Users should develop the habit of checking important information using trusted websites, books, teachers, or reliable sources. Fact-checking is especially important for school work, health advice, and serious decisions.

title: Critical Thinking with AI
content: AI should support thinking, not replace it. Critical thinking means analyzing information carefully, asking questions, and deciding whether something makes sense. Users should avoid accepting every AI response immediately and instead think about whether the answer is logical, accurate, and helpful.

title: Using AI for Learning
content: AI can be a powerful learning tool when used properly. Students can use AI to explain difficult topics, summarize ideas, generate practice questions, or brainstorm project ideas. However, learning is more effective when users understand the material instead of copying answers without thinking.

title: Privacy and Online Safety
content: When using AI tools, users should be careful about what information they share online. Personal details such as passwords, addresses, banking information, or private data should not be entered into AI systems. Understanding online safety helps users protect themselves while benefiting from technology.

title: Using AI Responsibly
content: Responsible AI use means being honest, careful, and thoughtful when using technology. People should avoid spreading false information, cheating, or depending too much on AI. The best way to use AI is as a helpful assistant that supports creativity, learning, and problem solving.
